summary: A custom kind lock-declared with a bare name colliding with a built-in silently loses its members in gate/explain/directive-classing — no diagnostic, no error

edit.description: Three identical sites — explain (~L428), gate (~L634), collect_directive_members (~L987) — iterate declarations.kinds and `if builtin_defs.contains_key(&row.name) { continue; }`, silently dropping a custom kind row whose bare name matches an embedded built-in's from every corpus (no admissibility check, no diagnostic, member_counts never populated for it). Verified (2026-07-08) this is the real collision mechanism — not check.rs:83's Workspace::load_kinds as the inbox report cited, which is only ever called with the fixed built-in-only definitions() set (main.rs:60) and so cannot actually receive a colliding custom-kind row. Consolidate the three duplicate checks into one shared partition helper (per specs/process/engineering.md, 'One job, one home') and, in gate's path, surface the collision as an admissibility-tier Diagnostic::error (pattern at check.rs:320-323) naming the colliding kind — refusing rather than silently skipping, so member loss is never silent. Rebase onto whatever resolve_kind_units/main.rs shape SATISFIES-GATE-FROM-LOCK leaves — same file, sequenced after it.

tests.asserts: a lock-declared custom kind whose name equals a built-in's (e.g. "rule") produces an admissibility-tier diagnostic naming the collision, rather than a silent zero-member drop

acceptance: A custom kind whose lock-declared name collides with an embedded built-in's is a well-formedness/admissibility finding, never a silent skip, across explain, gate, and directive-classing.
notes: Field report T16 (inbox, observed 4b0bb0f). Reporter flagged the exact coverage-summary render site ("command (0)") as untraced/unconfirmed and did not verify their cited check.rs:83 mechanism is reachable for this scenario — it is not; re-verification (2026-07-08) found the real mechanism at three sites in main.rs instead. scoped at 7cdf5ac.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, still at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Re-verified at 7cdf5ac (this tick): still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
